compiled_state.title: record-review.sh: missing --reviewer-hash documentation in error message -> agent must guess the call syntax
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
When record-review.sh is called without --reviewer-hash (the most common mistake), the error message shows only 'Usage: record-review.sh --reviewer-hash HASH [--expected-hash HASH]' without explaining where REVIEWER_HASH comes from or how to obtain it.

## Expected vs Observed
- **Expected**: Error message explains that REVIEWER_HASH is reported by the code-reviewer sub-agent in the REVIEWER_HASH= line of its output, and shows an example of the correct call.
- **Observed**: Generic usage message with no hint about where to get the hash value.

## Affected Component
plugins/dso/hooks/record-review.sh — error message for missing --reviewer-hash

## Notes
Filed from session friction — the orchestrator had to guess where REVIEWER_HASH came from. A one-line addition to the error message would prevent this.
